title: Small steps keep refactors bisectable
description: A sequence of small, individually-green refactor commits lets a regression be located by bisection, unlike one large mixed commit.
body: Use when planning how to sequence a larger refactor. Each step that compiles and passes tests on its own keeps history bisectable, so if a regression appears later git bisect lands on a small, understandable change; one giant commit collapses that signal. Do not batch many unrelated structural edits into a single commit. Actions: refactor in small steps, run the suite between each, and commit at green points. Validation signals include each commit passing tests and a clean bisect when hunting a later regression. Risk boundary: a large mixed commit makes regressions expensive to locate; keep steps small enough that any one is easy to revert.
